task_completion.description: Configure Wazuh FIM to monitor /var/www/html in real-time with report_changes enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing ossec.conf (or agent.conf) being edited or viewed, containing a syscheck block with `<directories realtime="yes" report_changes="yes">/var/www/html</directories>`.

task_completion.description: Implement a custom rule in local_rules.xml with ID 100050 and Level 12.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing local_rules.xml being edited or viewed, containing a rule tag exactly matching `<rule id="100050" level="12">`.

task_completion.description: Configure the custom rule as a child of rule 554 and set it to trigger on .php files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot showing the contents of the custom rule in local_rules.xml, which must include `<if_sid>554</if_sid>` and a condition matching .php files (e.g., `<match>.php</match>` or a PCRE2 regex for .php).

task_completion.description: Create a file named backdoor.php in /var/www/html to test the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal screenshot showing a command executed to create the file, such as `touch /var/www/html/backdoor.php` or `echo "..." > /var/www/html/backdoor.php`.

task_completion.description: Verify that the custom rule alert triggered for the creation of backdoor.php.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot of the Wazuh dashboard (Discover/Events) or terminal output of `/var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log` (or alerts.json) showing an alert for rule ID 100050 triggered by the file path `/var/www/html/backdoor.php`.

integrity.description: The agent restarted the Wazuh manager/agent to apply the configuration changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal screenshot showing a command like `systemctl restart wazuh-manager` or `/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart` after editing the configuration files.

integrity.description: The agent actually edited the configuration files rather than just echoing the expected text to the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Use of a text editor (nano, vim) or valid shell redirection/sed commands modifying `ossec.conf` and `local_rules.xml`.

integrity.description: The alert verification comes from actual Wazuh logs or the dashboard, not a manually created mock log file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert is viewed using commands like `tail`, `grep`, or `cat` on the actual `/var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log` or `alerts.json` file, or viewed within the Wazuh web UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target directory to monitor must be exactly '/var/www/html'. The custom rule in local_rules.xml must have ID '100050', level '12', and parent SID '<if_sid>554</if_sid>'. The agent must test the detection by creating a file named 'backdoor.php' in the target directory and verifying the alert.
